summary: Russia launched ballistic missile strikes on Kyiv and Kharkiv, with fires reported in Darnytskyi district and two injured in the capital. Ukrainian officials reported a mass drone attack on Chernihiv Oblast, wounding three people and damaging medical and educational facilities. Putin acknowledged strikes on Russian energy infrastructure are creating problems and discussed fuel market stabilization measures.
key_points: Russia launched ballistic missiles at Kyiv, with fires reported in Darnytskyi district and two people injured. (Source: @UkraineNow/82231)
A mass drone attack on Chernihiv Oblast wounded three people and damaged medical and educational facilities. (Source: @UkraineNow/82235)
Russia struck Zaporizhzhia, injuring eight people including two children, one a 5-year-old boy in serious condition. (Source: @UkraineNow/82244)
Putin acknowledged Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure are creating problems and discussed a possible full ban on diesel fuel exports. (Source: @tass_agency/382820)
Russian forces approached the settlement of Dobropillya, and Putin claimed no prepared defensive lines exist for Ukrainian forces beyond it. (Source: @tass_agency/382822)
Multiple UAVs were tracked across Kharkiv, Chernihiv, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, and Donetsk oblasts, with several heading toward Lozova, Pavlohrad, and Synelnykove. (Source: @kpszsu/66691)
Emergency power outages affected a significant portion of Zaporizhzhia Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382835)
Putin stated Russia expects American negotiators to arrive after the conclusion of the hot phase of events around Iran. (Source: @tass_agency/382827)

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched ballistic missile strikes on Kyiv and Kharkiv, with fires reported in Darnytskyi district and two injured in the capital. Ukrainian officials reported a mass drone attack on Chernihiv Oblast, wounding three people and damaging medical and educational facilities. Putin acknowledged strikes on Russian energy infrastructure are creating problems and discussed fuel market stabilization measures.

points: A mass drone attack on Chernihiv Oblast wounded three people, including a 48-year-old woman and a 72-year-old man with shrapnel injuries who were hospitalized, while one man declined hospitalization. Medical and educational facilities were damaged, as well as private homes and a vehicle. (Source: @UkraineNow/82235)
Russia struck Zaporizhzhia, injuring eight people including two children, one a 5-year-old boy in serious condition. (Source: @UkraineNow/82244)

Moldovan President Maia Sandu stated the withdrawal of Russian troops from Transnistria could be included in peace talks on Ukraine involving the EU, emphasizing that the illegal Russian military presence is the region's main problem. (Source: @UkraineNow/82242)

Zelensky announced plans to erect a full monument to Hetman Ivan Mazepa in central Kyiv on Shevchenko Boulevard, where Lenin's statue was toppled in December 2013, and a bust was already installed at Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra. (Source: @UkraineNow/82239)
Emergency power outages affected a significant portion of Zaporizhzhia Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382835)

Putin acknowledged Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure are creating problems, though he claimed they are not critical, and stated that part of the energy deficit will be covered by imports. (Source: @eRadarrua/98558)

Putin discussed a possible full ban on diesel fuel exports and acknowledged queues at gas stations with some fuel grades unavailable, while stating gasoline reserves stand at 1.7 million tons. (Source: @tass_agency/382801)
Traffic across the Kerch Bridge was temporarily closed. (Source: @tass_agency/382803)

In Miass, Chelyabinsk Oblast, a regional road at the 9th kilometer in the settlement of Chernovskoye-Ustinovo was washed out, and gasoline prices rose 1.5 times over several days. (Source: @eRadarrua/98517)

Putin stated Russia expects American negotiators to arrive after the conclusion of the hot phase of events around Iran, and that Russia was asked to make compromises formulated by American negotiators in Anchorage. (Source: @tass_agency/382827)
Russian forces approached the settlement of Dobropillya, and Putin claimed no prepared defensive lines exist for Ukrainian forces beyond it. (Source: @tass_agency/382822)
Multiple UAVs were tracked across eastern Ukraine, including heading toward Lozova in Kharkiv Oblast and Pavlohrad in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66691)
A ballistic missile was launched toward Kharkiv. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68157)
KABs were reported heading toward Prosyana in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast. (Source: @eRadarrua/98562)
UAVs were tracked near Novodonetsk in Donetsk Oblast on a northwesterly course. (Source: @kpszsu/66670)

Russia launched ballistic missiles at Kyiv, with fires reported in Darnytskyi district at several addresses including near a residential building, a car service station, and a non-residential building; two people were injured. (Source: @UkraineNow/82231)
A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches was reported from Bryansk Oblast and from Taganrog, Rostov Oblast. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68145)

Multiple UAVs were tracked across Chernihiv Oblast near Kholmy, Semenivka, Ponornytsia, Ichnia, Novhorod-Siverskyi, Pryluky, and Korop, including a Molniya-type UAV near Parafiivka/Ichnia. (Source: @eRadarrua/98532)
UAVs were tracked in Sumy Oblast near Shostka-Voronezh, Hlukhiv, and a reactive UAV circled near Krasnopillia, plus a reactive UAV on a course toward Terny. (Source: @eRadarrua/98571)
Russia struck Zaporizhzhia, injuring eight people including two children, one a 5-year-old boy in serious condition. (Source: @UkraineNow/82244)
Emergency power outages affected a significant portion of Zaporizhzhia Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382835)
UAVs were tracked in Mykolaiv Oblast toward Novyi Buh/Kazanka on a northwesterly course toward Kirovohrad Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66671)
UAVs were tracked in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast between Pavlohrad and Synelnykove, and near Pokrov on a northerly course. (Source: @kpszsu/66654)
Russia launched ballistic missiles at Kyiv, with fires reported in Darnytskyi district and two people injured. (Source: @UkraineNow/82231)
A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches was reported from Bryansk Oblast and from Taganrog, Rostov Oblast. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68145)

Multiple UAVs were tracked across Kharkiv, Chernihiv, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Donetsk, and Mykolaiv oblasts, including reconnaissance UAVs near Chuhuiv and reactive UAVs near Zmiiv and Lozova. (Source: @eRadarrua/98571)
KABs were reported heading toward Prosyana in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast. (Source: @eRadarrua/98562)
An air-to-air missile was reported in Sumy Oblast on a course toward Terny. (Source: @eRadarrua/98516)

key_developments: Russia launched ballistic missiles at Kyiv, with fires in Darnytskyi district and two injured, and a separate ballistic missile was fired at Kharkiv. (Source: @UkraineNow/82231)
A mass drone attack on Chernihiv Oblast wounded three people and damaged medical and educational facilities, private homes, and a vehicle. (Source: @UkraineNow/82235)
Russia struck Zaporizhzhia, injuring eight including two children, one a 5-year-old boy in serious condition. (Source: @UkraineNow/82244)
Putin acknowledged Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure are creating problems and discussed a possible full ban on diesel fuel exports. (Source: @tass_agency/382820)
Russian forces approached Dobropillya, and Putin claimed no prepared defensive lines exist for Ukrainian forces beyond it. (Source: @tass_agency/382822)
A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches was reported from Bryansk Oblast and from Taganrog, Rostov Oblast. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68145)
Putin stated Russia expects American negotiators to arrive after the conclusion of the hot phase of events around Iran. (Source: @tass_agency/382827)
sections.threat_assessment: The threat level remains escalating with ballistic missile strikes on Kyiv and Kharkiv, widespread UAV activity across multiple oblasts, and a high threat of Iskander-M launches from Bryansk Oblast and Taganrog. Russian forces are advancing near Dobropillya with Putin claiming Ukrainian defensive positions are lacking beyond it. The combination of strategic missile strikes on civilian infrastructure and continued drone campaigns indicates sustained Russian offensive pressure across all major axes.
sections.regional_response: Moldovan President Maia Sandu raised the prospect of including Russian troop withdrawal from Transnistria in EU-mediated peace talks on Ukraine. Zelensky announced a monument to Hetman Mazepa in central Kyiv, signaling continued nation-building efforts amid conflict. Emergency power outages in Zaporizhzhia Oblast reflect the downstream impact of strikes on energy infrastructure on civilian populations.
sections.intelligence_notes: Putin's public acknowledgment of energy infrastructure problems and fuel market instability represents a notable shift, as he discussed diesel export bans, 1.7 million tons of gasoline reserves, and government fuel stabilization headquarters operating around the clock. The Kerch Bridge traffic closure and road washout in Miass, Chelyabinsk Oblast, alongside fuel price spikes, suggest cumulative infrastructure strain. Putin's statements on Anchorage negotiations and expectations of American negotiators after the Iran situation indicate ongoing diplomatic maneuvering alongside military operations.
